tool_args.label: BUS RAPID TRANSIT PHASE II: Future Corridors
tool_args.raw_text: BUS RAPID TRANSIT PHASE II: Future Corridors. A New York City Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) planning study undertaken by NYC DOT and MTA New York City Transit.

payload.title: BUS RAPID TRANSIT PHASE II: Future Corridors
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation (DOT)
payload.description: Planning study of a citywide Bus Rapid Transit network, identifying 16 potential future BRT corridors for Phase II and short-listing three candidates for next SBS planning effort.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (DOT)
tool_args.raw_text: _NEW YORK CITY DOT_ — In the fall of 2008, the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and MTA New York City Transit (NYCT) initiated a planning study of a citywide Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) network.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation (DOT)
payload.description: City agency that co-led the BRT Phase II study and public outreach.

tool_args.label: MTA New York City Transit (NYCT)
tool_args.raw_text: _MTA New York City Transit_ — In the fall of 2008, the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and MTA New York City Transit (NYCT) initiated a planning study of a citywide Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) network.

payload.description: Transit agency that co-led the BRT Phase II study and public outreach.

tool_args.label: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey
tool_args.raw_text: the need to coordinate with the airport's operator, the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

payload.entity_name: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey
payload.description: Operator of LaGuardia Airport, requiring coordination for the LaGuardia/East Elmhurst corridor.

tool_args.label: BRT Phase II Study
tool_args.raw_text: In the fall of 2008, the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and MTA New York City Transit (NYCT) initiated a planning study of a citywide Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) network. This Phase II BRT study was undertaken based on the successful implementation of the Bx12 Select Bus Service (SBS) route, and the need to focus on cost-effective transit improvements... leading to the identification of 16 potential future BRT corridors.

payload.description: Citywide Bus Rapid Transit planning study initiated fall 2008 by NYC DOT and MTA NYCT, identifying 16 potential future BRT corridors based on transit needs analysis and public outreach.
payload.lead_agency: New York City Department of Transportation (DOT)

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

tool_args.label: BRT Phase II Study Initiated
tool_args.raw_text: In the fall of 2008, the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and MTA New York City Transit (NYCT) initiated a planning study of a citywide Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) network.

payload.description: NYC DOT and MTA NYCT initiated a planning study of a citywide Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) network.

tool_args.label: Introduction to Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Released
tool_args.raw_text: The results of the study are summarized in 'Introduction to Bus Rapid Transit Phase II,' which was released in April of 2009.

payload.description: Release of 'Introduction to Bus Rapid Transit Phase II' report summarizing results of the transit needs analysis.

tool_args.label: Public Workshops and Online Survey
tool_args.raw_text: In the spring of 2009 DOT and NYCT engaged New Yorkers in a dialogue on the future of BRT. This effort included seven public workshops across the city, along with an online survey. Over 325 people participated in the workshops...over 600 people completed the online survey.

payload.description: Seven public workshops across the city and an online survey. Over 325 people participated in workshops, over 600 completed the online survey.

tool_args.label: Bus Rapid Transit Phase II: Public Input Released
tool_args.raw_text: The results of the public outreach are summarized in 'Bus Rapid Transit Phase II: Public Input,' released in June of 2010.

payload.description: Release of 'Bus Rapid Transit Phase II: Public Input' report summarizing public outreach results.

tool_args.label: Webster Avenue/Third Avenue Corridor
tool_args.raw_text: Short List for Next Select Bus Service Route 1 Webster Avenue/Third Avenue Corridor ... NYCT and DOT have identified a short list of three candidates for the next BRT planning effort in New York City: Webster Avenue/Third Avenue in the Bronx

payload.description: Short-listed BRT Phase II corridor in the Bronx identified as a typical transit corridor with defined limits.

tool_args.label: Hillside Avenue Corridor
tool_args.raw_text: Short List for Next Select Bus Service Route 2 Hillside Avenue Corridor ... Hillside Avenue in Queens

payload.description: Short-listed BRT Phase II corridor in Queens identified as a typical transit corridor with defined limits.

tool_args.label: LaGuardia/East Elmhurst Corridor
tool_args.raw_text: Short List for Next Select Bus Service Route 3 LaGuardia/East Elmhurst Corridor ... the LaGuardia Airport/East Elmhurst Corridor is comprised of several inter-related travel markets

payload.description: Short-listed BRT Phase II corridor comprised of several inter-related travel markets tied together through one or more BRT services. Requires separate alternatives analysis and coordination with the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey.

tool_args.label: Evaluation Criteria for BRT Corridor Selection
tool_args.raw_text: The two agencies then evaluated these corridors on five primary criteria: community feedback, ridership potential, network benefits (i.e. connections to subway lines and other BRT routes), bus lane and busway feasibility, and geographic diversity.

payload.claim_text: NYCT and DOT evaluated over 40 potential BRT corridors on five primary criteria: community feedback, ridership potential, network benefits (connections to subway lines and other BRT routes), bus lane and busway feasibility, and geographic diversity.
payload.subject: BRT Phase II corridor evaluation
